behaviors.description: Plugin with missing required manifest field is rejected before any code from that plugin executes
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in the orchestration: does the loader validate AND prevent entry module import? A Unit test of the validator alone doesn't verify that entry code doesn't run. Requires validator + loader + mocked or real entry module to verify the full flow. Could mock filesystem for manifest.json, but can't mock the require() behavior without losing the core test.
behaviors.test_description: Create a manifest.json (or mock) missing a required field, attempt to load the plugin, verify: (1) loader raises validation error, (2) entry module's onLoad hook is NOT invoked, (3) entry module is not imported. Instrument entry module to verify it was not touched.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is appropriate: validator function is Unit, but orchestration (validator + loader + require prevention) is Integration.

behaviors.description: Plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's when A declares a dependency on B
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in dependency resolution + orchestration. A Unit test of a topological-sort algorithm alone doesn't verify the loader actually calls hooks in the resolved order. Must wire multiple real plugin modules together, have each declare dependencies, instrument their hooks to record call times, and verify sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create 3+ plugins with declared dependencies (e.g., A depends on B, B depends on C). Instrument each onLoad hook to record call timestamp or sequence. Load all plugins, verify hooks called in correct order: C → B → A. Capture call order via spy/mock callbacks.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Topological sort alone is Unit; but verifying orchestration (loader parsing deps + calling hooks in resolved order) requires Integration.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency between two plugins is detected and both plugins are rejected; neither onLoad hook is invoked
behaviors.justification: Real failure is twofold: (1) loader fails to detect cycle, leading to infinite loop or incorrect order, OR (2) loader detects cycle but loads plugins anyway. A Unit test of cycle-detection logic alone doesn't verify load prevention. Must wire two plugins with mutual dependencies through the full loader, verify no error OR error raised AND neither plugin loaded.
behaviors.test_description: Create 2 plugins with mutual dependencies (A → B and B → A). Attempt to load both. Verify: (1) loader raises cycle-detection error or warning, (2) neither plugin's onLoad hook is called, (3) entry modules are not imported. Record hook calls to confirm neither was invoked.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cycle-detection algorithm is Unit; orchestration (detecting + preventing load) is Integration.

behaviors.description: Plugin entry module is imported and executed before the plugin's onLoad hook is invoked
behaviors.justification: Real failure is timing: loader calls onLoad before require() finishes (especially with async imports). A Unit test of pure orchestration logic (if entry module required, then call hook) misses real import/execution timing issues. Must load a real entry module that sets a flag during import, then verify the flag is set before onLoad runs. Requires real or well-mocked require() behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets a global flag or module-level variable during import/execution. The plugin's onLoad hook reads this flag and would fail/throw if the flag is not set. Load the plugin, verify onLoad succeeds (flag is set), proving entry module executed before the hook. Test would fail if hook were called before import.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real entry module import/execution timing, not just mock orchestration logic. Integration level.

behaviors.description: When one plugin throws an error in its onLoad hook, loader continues loading remaining plugins that do not depend on the failed plugin
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in error recovery orchestration: does the loader correctly distinguish which plugins can load after a failure? A Unit test of error-handling logic alone doesn't verify partial-load behavior across multiple plugins with dependency relationships. Must load 3+ plugins where one fails, others depend on it or are independent, and verify selective loading based on dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create 3 plugins: (1) plugin A that throws in onLoad, (2) plugin B that depends on A, (3) plugin C that is independent. Load all three. Verify: (1) A's onLoad throws (caught by loader), (2) B is NOT loaded (depends on failed A), (3) C IS loaded (independent). Use hook instrumentation to confirm which plugins' onLoad hooks were actually called.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Error handling in isolation is Unit; orchestration (error isolation + dependency-aware skip decision + continued load) requires Integration.
